reviews.review_text: The easiest, affordable, safe, clean, comfortable and reliable accommodation we've experience in a very long time. Friendly and helpful staff.  Certainly great value for money!

We have  stayed here before and love it. We were on a large powered site with synthetic grass. It is dog friendly. The park is well kept and clean and the gardens well maintained. There is a swimming pool and  kids water park. The park is located on the river with lovely walking areas. It in a good location and  close access to everything. The staff are great.

Amazing location to take your boat and fish! The on site boat ramp was the best in the area and served great ease.
Unfortunately we forgot our soap and were denied being given one from reception because we were camping not in cabins so dont forget your soap! Overall awesome stay!
Beautiful Location, Awesome Staff and Clean as a whistle
Very impressed with the amenities, decent size site for caravan & vehicle & wonderful friendly staff, however too many non Sea Eagle supporters lol

We have stayed here a couple times now, the park is very clean, the staff are extremely friendly, & we always feel comfortable staying there. In fact I have already booked for two more stays for the remainder of 2026.
Stayed art the van park, great facilities, awesome staff.

They answer the phone better than Port Macquarie, otherwise I'd be in Port tonight!!
Beautiful holiday park tucked away on a promontory - a genuine waters' edge property. Quiet, away from the road; boat ramp, marina nearby. This has got to be Twin Towns best kept secret!!
Smooth, efficient reservation, payment and after hours key collection process, members' discount. This place has a water park, swimming pool and lap pool, varied accommodations, I checked out the caravan sites which are 100% Seagrass matting, so no worn out, bumpy, uneven grass or muddy feet! I'd struggle getting my 21 footer in as straoght as these ones! I hope they help you!! Dog friendly.
Cabin 11, awesome, new-ish, I love it when you come in off the road & the air con and lights are on welcoming you like home.
Queen bed and 2 bunks, small bathroom with plastic shower recess with step and no grab rails. Perfectly provisioned, good sized kitchen with microwave, quality thoughtful, generous range of utensils and good sized fridge.  I'll be back but might think twice about bringing the van!! Next time Studio 6. $120 per night, economical, small, no room for suitcases. Otherwise perfect for  single or couple but not for a long stay. Well provisioned kitchen, great shower recess,great hot water.

Great big sites, astro turf, plenty of room. Great location right next to the river, close to shops,  pubs and clubs. Great fishing and swimming beaches. Nice pool with slide for kids. Nice friendly staff, option for them to use tractor t park vans in tricky spots.
If you want to hear screaming out of control kids all day for 100's of metres around the park and surrounding areas this is the park for you!

We have stayed at this park on three trips now, and have been very happy on every occasion. The amenities are clean and modern, the staff are wonderful and friendly, the pool and kitchen are great also. Highly recommend this park.

I don’t really care if they know who I am because as far as I’m concerned $281 is a lot of money for a tiny little camper van. You think that the site would be gold and then you got yap and dogs all night and people are up all all night talking kids screaming carrying on can’t get into town when I walked over to the cafe. It was closed. I miss the ice cream guy twice you didn’t even come down our lane so I won’t be going back and I definitely won’t be telling anyone to Go there I could’ve stayed in a high-rise apartment for that sort of money. They’re just rip-offs charging whatever they want to.
Great location, very friendly and helpful staff, large sites with synthetic grass, facilities and amenities are keep clean and well maintained, enjoyed our stay 👍👍👍

This is the best park we have ever stayed in. Friendly, efficient staff. Spotless amenities. Level, clean sites. Three pools, one for the toddlers, one general with water slide and one for adults. Fishing on the lakeside with bbq facilities nearby. Cafe on site with delicious, reasonably priced meals. Also,  food vans came into the park to offer pizzas, Thai, baked potatoes and the prawn man comes too.  The park puts on a free sausage sizzle once a week. Why would you want to leave?  Close to retail therapy as well.  10 out of 10 for this park. Will come back
Just used the swimming pool. It was Fun

Loved my stay here at the NRMA Holiday Caravan Park.
Amazing receptionists, friendly and helpful.
Very clean and my cabin had a well equipped kitchen.
I would highly recommend this caravan park.
Totally clean and every caravan spot has synthetic grass!

We had a great time at our family holiday! Staff were so friendly and helpful ,
Lots of things to do around here !
Cabins were clean and nice just wished the main bed would’ve been abit bigger !
Powered sites clean and easy to set up. Staff friendly and helpful, boat ramp in good condition, cleaning tables handy and in good condition.

Booked in for a two night stay in a Studio cabin. The room was spacious and beautifully clean, the bed was a pillow top and very comfortable.  The pillows not so great. There was parking close by, and you could wander down to the lake in less than 30 seconds. The park is very neat and tidy, with its own boat ramp, pools, and an on-site Cafe, which you get a two for one coffee voucher for when you arrive.
CONS: no privacy in the room, even opening the bathroom window is impossible because people in nearby cabins can see in.
Terrible TV issues. The first night it didn't work at all,  but we could listen to next door's TV quite clearly! Got someone to fix the problem the next day, but then that night, again, the TV stops working.  Called the Emergency number and a nice man turned up and fixed the issue, again. It was just before 8pm and he was just knocking off, so luckily we could relax and watch a movie.
Lastly,  the Cafe on site was horrible.  It was just after 1.30pm when we ordered but they were already trying to pack up to close at 2pm. The chicken schnitzel burgers were tasteless and the chicken deep fried and slightly burnt.  I had a decaf coffee with soy milk that I could not drink, it was horrible and burnt tasting. When our half finished meal was collected and we were asked how it was, I just couldn't say "great" because it wasn't.  The lady's reaction? Well it filled a hole at least!
This is why I don't holiday in Australia much, because good food makes a holiday special.

This NRMA operated caravan park and cabins is well managed. What is unique is an onsite cafe that opens for breakfast and lunch. Coffee is quality and well made. Staff are excellent.|I single bedrooms the shower cubicle is the smallest I have endured in my world travels. Over 90kg you couldn’t fit! Design is to produce a toddlers bath.|Old cabins well maintained

Location:
-away from the busy road and by the lake. Very good location. You will need a car to get into town though

Rooms:
-we stayed in 2 bed cabin. The rooms were clean, kitchen had all the utensils needed and the furniture was decent. Plantation shutters were helpful.
-I've stayed at other parks and this is one of the few that doesn't feel dated.

Amenities:
-common showers and toilets were dated but kept clean
-great hot water and pressure
-pool was great and the slides was lots of fun. Please note the pool is supposed to be heated but this was variable. It's not that is was cold it was just hotter on some days and cooler on other days.
-the provided floatation jackets for little ones was a nice touch
-bbq facilities were reasonably clean
-the boardwalk by the lake was excellent and good for walks and subsets. Beware the pelicans.

Staff:
-Pretty helpful staff who communicated well. One thing that was odd is that we asked for more toilet paper and dish soap and were told it is only provided on the first night and afterwards not provided. I didn't respond and eventually they did provide it but i thought that this was a bizarre thing as we were staying in a cabin and not in a caravan.
Forster is our new favourite place. Such a great caravan park in the best location, we've already booked to come back next year!

Very Disappointed and Frustrated.
I’m extremely disappointed with the service I received. I called today to book a 3-night stay in December, and the first staff member I spoke with confirmed everything — gave me all the details, the total cost, and said it was perfectly fine. I told her I’d call back in a few minutes once I had my card ready.
When I called back, a different staff member answered and told me there’s a 7-night minimum for those dates. I was shocked and frustrated — why would one staff member tell me one thing and another completely contradict it?
As an NRMA member who has stayed at this park before, I expected better communication and consistency. This was supposed to be our only little holiday over Christmas, and it’s been completely ruined because of this poor handling. Very disappointing experience!

Powered site on artificial grass.
Clean amenities, great cafe, and fantastic water park and pool.
Family favourite,  friendly holiday makers, good vibe

The perfect place to stay with kids. So much for the kids to do around the park with pool, water park, fishing, bike riding, playground and volleyball. Plus school holiday activities. Only a short 2km over to Forster to enjoy all the amenities. Close to golf courses, beaches, boat hire, putt putt and more. The 2 bedroom cabin was cosy and comfortable. Nice big shower, clean linen and dishes. We found everything to be great. My only complaint is that the floors in the cabin were quite dirty. The dark floors don't show the dirt but the bottom of our feet were black after only a few min walking barefoot around the cabin. The pots and pans could also use an upgrade. But loved the comfy couch, plantation shutters, tables and chairs were all quality. We hope to bring the kids back again. 😊

We stayed here in the recent school holidays. The park was amazing with multiple activities each day for the kids to keep them occupied. The pool and water area is great. The communal showers were great, the water was always hot and the water pressure was strong. The staff are very helpful and friendly. Highly recommended for a family friendly holiday.
Great place, nice and quiet, own boat ramp, outstanding accommodation,  friendly staff. Easy walk to beach and break wall.

Really enjoyed having one of our bikes stolen from our caravan. Locks and bike rack cut. Management informed, didn’t alert other guests to the risk considering it was the second theft of the day. Secure your site!

Facilities and the park really well maintained, lots for the kids to do. Excellent location with direct water access. Astro turf sites are awesome. Reception should inform guests when arriving that they will park your van for you, most people we saw throughout the week were halfway through parking their van when the tractor turned up, including us.

Nice holiday park having camped here for 5 nights. The site and facilities were excellent. I liked the "Marriage Saver" tractor service which enabled easy positioning of caravans on site. No doubt saves a few marriages.

This is a very good holiday park! It has all of the amenities you need, it's in a pretty good location and to top it off the views across the lake are gorgeous! We stayed in a two bedroom cabin and it was a very good size for the five of us! It had a tv, powerful shower, oven/grill, microwave fridge and a dishwasher! All in all it was a great experience and would come back again!

We stayed in a 2 bedroom cabin 2 adults 3 children and really enjoyed our 4 night stay here. We would definitely recommend family's to come stay here.

2 bedroom with queen bed and other room had double bunk beds. The living area was spacious with 6 chairs around the table and large sofa for all of us to sit on and smart TV with WiFi access so if it rained we could stay in and connect. WiFi was really good for a holiday park - occasionally dropped out but wasn't too bad.

The amenities are really good if you are camping, plenty of clean toilets and showers BBQs and a camp kitchen.

The games room is great with a few free classic arcade games, table tennis  and some paid games machines.

Great board walk for fishing or relaxing and bait is sold at the on site cafe from 7am-3pm

The swimming pool for children was fantastic and our children loved going on the slide over and over again.

We will definitely be coming back again, thank you for a great stay 😀
We were in our caravan.
Great place to visit.
Definitely will love to come back. Evething was tidy and clean.

Incredible service, lovely and friendly. The cabins are clean and spacious and the park is well maintained and open.

Plenty of pelicans and even dolphins hanging out along the waters edge.

Come for relaxation and stay for the sunsets!

Fantastic service here. Thoroughly enjoyed our stay !!
Would definitely come back 🌟
Nice place, but someone deliberately scratched our car right along one side, so not that impressed.

We stayed here for 4 nights in Aug '25.  This is our second visit (the first was a 1-night stop over on a northern road trip).  Our site was a powered ensuite site (E180).  The site was a smaller one, but perfect for our teardrop camper with plenty of room to set up both a 270 deg. awning out one side/back and a 2.5 m awning the other, with room for the car to park in front.  The ensuite was great!  The park was nice and quiet, despite having quite a few bookings.  The amenities were also very good, and the camp kitchen was likewise very good with plenty of room to cook and clean.  There's a lot to see in the Forster Tuncurry area, and if you're looking for a base, this is worth a look.
Beautiful little coastal town, great value and really friendly staff at reception. Clean facilities, cute cabins and great pool areas
Thi is easily the most well maintained park that I have stayed at. Staff are friendly and helpful.
Great park with excellent amenities. I would stay away from the Sudio Cabins. There are shared walls and every noise can be heard from the adjacent rooms.

We stayed here last week as an emergency after our other bookings got cancelled due to floods.They were amazing!They got us in straight away in a beautiful pet friendly cabin as we were travelling with our cavoodle  .The place was immaculate,the staff amazing !! The location fantastic. Nothing was a bother.It was so good we extended our stay .We will be back 5 stars aren't enough
Was great stayed in a self contained cabin was perfect for our 4 person family (2 adults, 2 kids). Great amenities at the premise as well. Front desk/ reception staff were always happy and helpful.
Great Park all staff extremely helpful and the park is a credit to all involved
I must say it was the cleanest cabin

This would be a great place for families with young kids, which l don't have. I didn’t find it suitable for my style with my 2 dogs. It was expensive and fake grass onsites, just not my thing, but a clean park. Stayed one night, then headed off to a bush campground, which cost less for 3 nights than the one night here.

Love our stay. It was great for our dog too. He was left a cute envelope of treats/doggy bag just for him. Very comfortable and clean cabin on the water. Super relaxed after this short getaway. Thank you.

Is a great place to be with your family and couple. They have many common areas and also private studios. You can came with your own camper van too.
The have a lovely lake next to the accommodation and a food truck as well. And they are pet friendly!!

The staff could not have done more to make our stay more comfortable! We called up beforehand to ask about dust (one has an allergy) and heating. The lady on the phone let us know they would note the dust allergy with house keeping. We arrived and discovered the room was sparkling clean and they had even put on the heating for us so the place was warm. On a cool winter evening, the warm room was especially welcome. When I spoke to the staff the following morning, it was clear they were genuinely concerned for our comfort. Professional and kind! The room was also very nice and modern.
Very clean park, and we would stay here again.
Nice place to stay particularly if you have a pet.

Great Caravan Park! Awesome location. Amazing service - due to a hospitalisation over Easter (silly accident) had to cancel our booking last minute, given credit which was great so we can revist soon!
Great stay in my caravan with my dog 🐕,  quiet and looking over Omaha waterway. 2 boat ramps available also. Love the price and 3rd day special NRMA member.

This park is definitely up there with the more superior parks. Friendly professional staff, well managed. Very generously sized sites, well maintained garden beds, artificial turf throughout. No sloshing through mud and puddles when it rains. There are two large amenities which are always spotless, park bins are emptied twice a day, the artificial turf is regularly vacuumed. The location on the lake is fabulous. There’s a boat ramp, short boardwalk, lots of picnic tables, large fish cleaning area. Perfect spot for boating, kayaking, SUP and fishing. 15 Minute flat walk to Woolworths, a lovely 25 minute flat walk along the lake and harbour to Rockpool Cafe, pool and breakwater. This walk is particularly beautiful as the sun is coming up. We really loved how quiet the park is being tucked away from the hustle and bustle of the town but still within walking distance to everything.

A fantastic park. The staff are helpful, friendly and very dog friendly. The sites are large and first class. The amenities are spotless at all times.
Fantastic location, close to town and cafes.
Well done guys, just a shame the weather was absolutely crap. Although the sites are high and dry and extremely well drained.

The cabin was good, a fishy smell probably due to previous occupants cooking or gutting fish in there.a little bit dated. It had fridge, gas cook top and microwave. Close to the beach and lake. Close to restaurants and eateries. 5minute drive to shops. Friendly staff.

We have stayed in a few cabins in caravan parks and this one tops the lot!
Very friendly staff, easy to get to, great location near the water and above all cleanliness/ fabulous!! Thank you for accommodating us 5 stars for everything!
Absolutely loved this place. A great place to relax with family and friends.
Nice relaxing place

An amazing park. Awesome helpful and friendly staff. We were worried about being away from beach and shops and dining BUT they provided a list of restaurants with courtesy bus contacts. Cafe on site with text sent for daily specials. Sausage sizzle welcome and meet and greet and Woodfire pizza by the water. This park is very accomodating and well maintained whilst having abundant facilities to accomodate from couples to families. Just awesome. Thoroughly recommend 🤩
This caravan park is one of the best we have been to plenty of space between the vans and road and the best thing they park your van for you 👍never had this before.
Stayed in our caravan.  Great sites! Good facilities
Cabins are fantastic and have everything you need, and the grounds are kept clean and tidy. A great spot next to the lake, and pet friendly.

About $160 per night for a camp site over Easter break (crazy, but we still paid it).
Clear rules saying no e-scooters or e-bikes, but there was plenty of them being ridden. Staff walked past not caring.
Showers are moldy, taps in sinks were broken, toilets clogged. Sign on bathroom door said cleaned twice a day but they weren’t.
Originally asked for a quiet spot (were given one at the back), but when we got there we were right next to the pool. While it was great everyone’s kids were having a good time, it was very loud all day.
Rules regarding no swimming/ bikes/ scooters at night were ignored and staff did nothing.
Lots of dogs barking at all hours. Need caravan/ camping section with no dogs.

Awesome location
Caravan spots
Cabins
Boat ramps
Swimming pool
Relaxing grass areas, etc, etc
Thanks for having us

The park itself is well kept and clearly recently upgraded. Having stayed in similar properties in the area the general ambience and quality is superior. Staff were welcoming and there was even a complimentary sausage sizzle in the early evening. My only caution would be to avoid the 'studio cabins' as they are three separate rooms under one roof, which appears to be a multi sided cabin structure. I sit here in the room and can hear quite clearly the fellow in the next room, and the woman in the other room who has spent the last three hours on the phone talking quite loudly to her friends.
It would appear the rooms are divided by a couple of sheets of gyprock filled with some kind of rudimentary sound proofing material which is barely effective. I would suggest stretching the budget to one of the cabins which share no common walls.
Following morning the management graciously moved us to a cabin for our final night.
This is very nice and we are grateful for the speedy solution done with courtesy - I cannot fault the efforts of staff to resolve our issue.

0ne of the best caravan parks we have been in.|From the friendly staff, to the tractor who puts each van on there site.|All sites are turfed with artifical grass and each site is vacumed when you leave.|This is a dog friendly park and plenty of space to walk your dog.|The pool area is amazing and they even a  full range of life jackets for kids to wear. There is a volley ball caught and games room.|You can still down to the water located in park and have a picnic or fish off the bank. I saw a large flathead and bream caught. Only downside ..beware of the pelicans who snatched a fish off Mt line as I was trying to remove hook and took a piece out of my hand.|All in all great park and we will be back

The team at NRMA took our last minute booking request (the day before) and managed to fulfill our needs with a gorgeous villa by the river. The park was fantastic and my children had a wonderful time exploring the region. I would highly recommend a stay here for that perfect family getaway!
Had an ensuite van site for 2 nights. Great to have a tractor to reverse the van in as the spots are tight. Friendly staff and clean amenities

My first time here was my partner and our dog and had cabin #14 facing the lake. It was gorgeous. Clean, easy to use, everything worked, especially the AC and Dog friendly.
We were here for a family gathering & reunion. Staff were very friendly, amenities were good, and they definitely will come back. Highly recommend. We will definitely come back.
THANK NRMA Foster Tuncurry caravan Park.

Stayed 6nights in Powered caravan site. Had an excellent experience, good amenities, close to the water, boat ramp, great swimming pool and camp kitchen. Was welcomed by friendly staff on entry. All in all it was a terrific experience.
Only stayed 1 night but it seemed nice enough. The dryer did steal my money though which p'd me off.
This caravan park is so well looked after it has 2 pool,is right on the river and the rooms are clean and there are plenty of sights and cabins to choose from.

Absolutely loved this caravan park.
Clean, tidy, great service and staff, perfect location highly recommend.

We have a Kelpie who is an outside dog most of time and most cabins we stay at are soooooooo restricted as where you can have your best mate.
This park has used their brains and have cabins facing a council reserve.
If my dog is happy im happy. I'll definitely be back. Loved the stay cabins were great. Really had a great time

Beautiful family campsite wonderful staff. Great concept to have pop-up food tents.
Pizza and dutch pancakes were great!
Had a fantastic weekend away at the NRMA Forster Tuncurry Holiday Park. The kids pool and facilities were amazing!
Fabulous caravan park, boat ramp, cafe, great staff, every site has artificial grass, a must visit, now getting very expensive though
Great holiday park. Staff welcoming, friendly and helpful.
Beautiful location
The staff and management are very friendly and efficient.
Was only visiting to charge my car but looked amazing
Our family had the best time here over Christmas week 2024. The staff were amazing and there were plenty of in house activities for the kids - Santa on Christmas Eve was a big hit!!
The onsite people think they own the place playing music to all hours of the night kids are screaming trying to sleep fireworks going off and security just sit by the foreshore
Early December was hot when we set up , it's a dog friendly park with large sites for our large motorhome,  camp.kitchen was clean and had plenty of cooking areas

Amazing holiday park for kids 😺🎉 🥳
It is a Christmas holiday, so most camper side was very crowded. 😁 Overall is great holiday park😊
Even though wifi connected, it was not working!!!
Disappointing service
Good park but tight & they park vans for you, boat ramp 50m from sites
No facilities- splash pad closed with LOUD work going on, cafe closed, jumping pillow gone, smoke alarms expired and going off randomly, not suitable for kids

We have stayed on the powered site a few times now and just love it. The grounds and everything around is always kept clean and tidy. The staff at reception are always very helpful.  Great facilities.
Fabulous caravan park!!! Really clean and kid friendly! Great amenities

Stayed for a few days in a 2 bedroom cabin. Super comfy beds. Kitchen and lounge room were clean. Facilities were good; please note the jumping pillow seen in the photos no longer exists. Broke the little one’s heart, but that’s ok.

The holiday park overlooks the Coolongolook River, which is beautiful at sunset. Lots of fish and Pelicans. I’m told there are dolphins sometimes, too. Overall very nice.

It’s a busy park and you can see why.

Close to beaches and the rest of town.
Amazing location, not far from Forster. Bungalow in great condition with every things you need!

Would’ve loved to have stayed here but unfortunately couldn’t even get past the booking process.
I called up as advised on the website because we were booking with pets and wanted to make sure that our pitches were next to each other.
The lady I spoken to previously was very nice and said that we could have 14 people across 2 pitches as 2 of them were toddlers and didn’t count.
This time however the lady said that 6 per pitch was the max occupancy and the toddlers did count. That was fine with us as 2 adults in our group hadn’t actually confirmed yet so I said we would proceed with the 12 people across 2 pitches. For some reason this was a massive no. A lot of back and forth with the fact that she would have to take me at my first request of 14 people and couldn’t accept when I was telling her that the other 2 people would book their own pitch if they decided to join us.
At one point I was even asked how the 2 toddlers would book their own pitch? Which made 0 sense as the 2 extra people were adults and we would never put 2 three year olds on their own.
I asked to speak to someone else and was told no, placed on hold for a while and then told the same thing that she wouldn’t let me book 2 pitches for 14 people - again, not what I was asking for anymore. I was essentially called a liar, she must’ve thought I wanted to sneak the other 2 people in, despite me saying multiple times that they would book their own if they decided to join. Very frustrating.
We have now booked elsewhere which is a shame as we were all excited about this place.
Great park

Amazing park! Has a great kids pool and an adult pool also. They did heaps of great activities in the school holidays also. The streets were concrete so kids could ride bikes and scooters easily. Lots of amenities out though that was the only down fall. Loved staying here. Location was amazing too
Good location and dog freindly which was a deal breaker for us.
The best
Great location and amazing sites. The Marriage Saver tractor a bonus. Staff very helpful and friendly
Sites were immaculate. Great stay midweek kayaking and saw dolphins & an osprey catch a big fish to share with its fledglings.

Booked in with my partner on a caravan site. Had to be put in via a tractor but assured when we wanted to leave to just ring the office. We rang -  put on hold for over10 min - went to the office- waited another 10 min before was asked what I want - then I was told “I’m on the phone and wait”. Older lady in Reception needs to learn to treat her customers with respect or get out of the people business. 🤨So rude!  Park overall was a good one. Experience at reception was appalling.

The park is beautifully maintained, well laid out with amenities easy to access. There are three different swimming pools, a couple for the kids and a lap pool for the adults. Situated right on Lake Wallis, it’s the perfect spot for fishing and kayaking or paddle boarding. The best bit is it’s pet friendly and you can check in with your four-legged fur babies. |We were in a 2 bedroom cabin which was fresh and clean and quite spacious. There’s a good mix of studio, one bedroom, two bedroom, three bedroom and powered caravan sites. The park’s “marriage saver” aka tractor will park caravans in position for you.
We are on an 8 week trip staying in a different place every week. This is by far our favourite spot so far.

As soon as we arrived we booked another night. This park is absolutely amazing 🤩. Pool, pet friendly, cabin was perfect fully equipped. Staff were friendly and dinner nights are a fantastic idea. Close proximity to everything. We will be back!!! 👌

What a terrific Park.  The sited ha e plenty of room. They have Happy Hour, a great play area and pool for the kids and you can also take a 2 min walk from your van to fish in the river.  Very clean amenities. Very impressed with this park.

We did a short visit this time in a cabin.
Last stay we had stuff stolen even being locked up.
This time our neighbours had their boat motor and 2 bikes stolen while they slept. Also locked up.
Now it's been many years we have been visiting and it seems to be every weekend the locals come to clean up what isn't theirs.
It makes me feel very uncomfortable, violated and in some ways very unsafe.
As much as we love this beautiful park and it just doesn't feel good anymore.

Great park, booked 7x stayed 10x on powered caravan site, some rows have larger sites than others depending on where you are but  tractor puts you on site. Shout out to the tractor team, the maintenance staff for beautiful grounds & sites, cleaners & reception, delightful staff all round & it shows in the park. Had couple visiting food vans & sausage sizzle night. Great camp kitchen & pool area. Park was busy in July but still felt you had your own space. Tables & chairs on waterfront. Cafe right next door open wed-sun excellent bacon & eggs. Highly recommend.

What a gem of a park!  It was one of the cleanest and tidiest parks I've ever been too.  The staff were always working outside in the gardens, on the sites, in the bathrooms and pools and taking garbage.  The 2 bedroom cabin with a bath in the bathroom was clean.  We had 2 adults and 2 children.  We arrived just after the heaviest downpours of rain for a whole week had occurred.   We were allowed to exchange our towels every day for clean towels as well as obtain more blankets.  We did this so as not to keep damp towels in the cabin which after the 2nd use weren't drying nicely.  We had a balcony with outdoor setting, a 6 seater dining table and a nice kitchen, with Microwave, oven & stove.  All the amenities if you needed to cook. There is an onsite cafe and mini store.  Not open every day though.  Made really nice coffee and the kids loved their milkshakes.  We went with a group of friends and in the evenings, we would go down to the BBQ area down on the lake and throw a line in to fish in the lake.  We only caught little fish due to so much fresh water from the rain, though it kept us entertained!  On the last night we  went up to the amazing camp kitchen which had a couple of ovens, stove tops, microwaves and BBP Plates, as well as chairs and tables. Has a swimming pool with a slide and then another little water play area with 2 smaller slides for the smaller kids.  and a Spa.  Also has an adult only pool to swim laps. Also has a good-sized boat ramp to launch boats and a jetty the length of the park on the riverfront to tie boats up on. I wish we were able to stay longer, though we only had small amount of time off work.  I can't wait to go back and visit and stay longer next time!  We loved the holiday and would love to go back.
Caravan Park , very well run . Clean everything . Beautiful spot .
Fabulous area to the side to run your dogs. Such a clean and peaceful place to holiday

We stayed 2 nights in a caravan sites with a ensuite.
It was clean tidy and fully equipped with towels etc.
The park has the best camp kitchen 2 fully equipped kitchens with oven hot plates with plates , cutlery,  cups , wine glasses. Just need to bring your food.
Large swimming pool and water slide .
This is a great holiday park.

Don't miss this! Incredible location, super family and dog friendly, great fishing. Staff are all amazing and friendly- including the cafe staff. Heaps of on-site entertainment facilities and super close to local shops, restaurants and attractions

Great pet friendly park in a beautiful location. We stayed in a lake view 2BR cabin in July. The cabin was clean and the park was well maintained. The lake was just off our cabins and we enjoyed canoeing there. It was very convenient.
Friendly staff.
A very well appointed park. Lots to do for the kids. Gorgeous river frontage and entertainment and sausage sizzle was a bonus. Beautifully maintained and quiet.

We stayed here for three nights in a studio cabin. Nice clean accommodation. Just a couple of things that need attention:||1. Blankets on the queen bed instead of a doona. Heavy and scratchy and not warm enough.||2. The TV started to randomly turn itself off and go back to an app menu - a bit annoying.||3. Attempted to use the laundry but all the dryers in both laundries were out of order.||Otherwise a nice stay and would return.

So impressed by this park! We could tell it was recently renovated, the staff were so friendly and helpful, the amenities were great and having synthetic was awesome. The whole park was clean and the facilities were all so good! We will definitely be back. One of the nicest and cleanest parks we’ve been to.
Great park friendly staff and clean amenities. I highly recommend staying here .
This place is 10/10

3 bedroom villa with a waterview was beautiful! It’s like you’d wanna stay there forever! Had lots to do around the park pools, bike hire,volley ball, a park for the kids also so convenient with things to do like the rock pools, cinema, tuncurry shops and the beach & Forster shops is a short drive it is such a great location to stay would recommend anyone to stay here!

This park has so much to offer |Valet parking by the marriage saver tractor and those guys are great |The park is immaculate and very spacious nice views of the lake also |They have different things like sausage sizzle. Pizzas and always receive a text when they are on |The office staff always helpful and polite. Cleaners do a great job. The gardeners and tractor drivers are fantastic |We will certainly be back
Enjoyed staying in the lakeside cabin, nice and quiet ,relaxing.

We just spent three nights here and loved it. First time / wonderful customer service. Love love loved the tractor service. Was very quick and efficient. Sites were beautiful, clean and spacious astroturf. We had lovely neighbours. Amenities were cleaned regularly. A little too far to walk into town with our dog and young child but only a short drive and worth it. We hired a pontoon boat on lake Wallis to watch the dolphins - highly recommend

Absolute top spot to stay when visiting Forster Tuncurry. Nice quiet park with a homey feel. All team were amazing and very accomodating, they made us feel very welcome, especially both Kelly’s. Amenities and the room we stayed in were spotless. BBQ’s spotless. We highly recommend staying here!! We will be back!! :)

Wow absolutely brilliant we have just had the best week holiday you could not fault this holiday park in any way.  Everything was so clean.  Staff amazing. For those who don't like backing they even have a free backing service . Thanks
Such nice people great service beautiful location

Always a great place to stay. We had some very unruly people in a large group spoiling it last week for all with loud noise and swearing until 2:30 am The park wasted no time dealing with the problem and moving them on . The park is always well kept and drains really well during heavy rain .

Top-notch amenities, including large and spotless ablutions, grass sites, slab sites, ensuite sites and a large pool & water-play complex.
BBQ areas with electric barbeques dotted throughout the park, an
excellent camp kitchen.... the list goes on and on!
They can even come and park your van for you!

Kid friendly? More like a retirement village!
We had a complaint at 6pm about our kids being too loud on their bikes, all under 5 and just happily playing outside of our own campsite & cabins supervised by adults we had 3 sites and 7 cabins.
Later that night there was a complaint about some of the group sitting on one of the sites talking and instead of a friendly approach they were told to go inside and go to bed....there were empty campsites around us and the couple in the van closest to us said they didn't hear a thing.
The following morning I recieved a text message saying we were being evicted from the park due to the incidents and displayed behaviour, despite our clamping tent being at the opposite end of the park they just evicted everyone who's booking was attached to the group including grandparents. Which was totally unfair to those who weren't involved.

It's such a same because the amenities and my glamping tent were great, my daughter was devastated that she didn't get to stay in "her tent"

Thankfully we were able to find alternative accommodation at Big 4 Manning Point where we have had no trouble and we could sit up talking til we were ready for bed.

I wish I could give 0 stars for this place, Max the manager is a rude and arrogant disrespectful man. Kicked out over kids playing at 6pm at night. Given an hours notice to leave the park at checkout time and threaten to call police all over kids having fun on holidays. The cabins are very overpriced for what they are. Overall worst experience I’ve ever had at a caravan park and 100% would not go back or recommend this place to anyone.
Great caravan park, large pitch, very clean toilet block, excellent camp kitchen with everything you need, superb swimming pool.

Neat and tidy park in a good central location.

The staff at NRMA Forster Tuncurry were polite, courteous and friendly.
Maybe they could teach the staff at NRMA Port Macquarie a thing or two about good customer service.
Such friendly staff and great location

Well organised and good overall environment. Teeming with kids on scooters and bikes in the Easter school holidays but security and safety were all good.
School holiday activities included waterfront activities and pancakes. Stayed for under a week and enjoyed the stay.
Amenities kept clean and tidy.
Kids 11 & 13 spent a lot of time in the pool with the slide being very fun. Smaller section of the pool area suitable for smaller tots and plenty of room for seating for supervision.
Lots of play equipment and a beach volleyball court near the common areas.
Check in was good as we were able to pre sign everything and valet parking the caravan was great.
Funny that the valet tractor was named "marriage saver".
The e bikes for hire were in good condition, charged, and available when we wanted them, and cheaper than the beam public scooters

It's a nice park but the kids run amuck there's no policing for music late at night or kids on their scooters and bikes - hanging around and playing with the boom gates. I felt like this was such a big park with over 200 sites but we were all on top of each other and the amenities just can't keep up with a full house. The checkin process was easy but don't think about leaving at 2pm bcuz you'll have both lanes backed up with the leaving lane being used for people to enter to.
Good location with its own boat ramp.

Lovely well kept park
BUT
Almost half of people staying were celebrating the end of Ramadan & many had no consideration for anyone who was just staying for a quiet getaway and did not adhere to the 10pm curfew. At night there were tribes of kids out being raucous and carrying on until someone reined them in at abt 11pm.
Neighbour informed us that the adult pool was trashed by kids and had to be closed for cleaning next day. Kids were playing soccer everywhere & bouncing soccer balls off vans and the adults gathering in large groups talking at the top of their voices. One couple we spoke to had to move their van to another area because the noise became unbearable. Personally..  we put some music on (just radio station) quietly only to be drowned out deliberately by people nearby with their kind of music. We were told of some other awful incidents too but they didn’t affect us. If only we’d known the majority of people were here to celebrate and be so inconsiderate to those who weren’t, we would never have booked especially as it was so expensive. $418.50 for a powered site for 4 nights .
Sorry we won’t be back . Once bitten twice shy!

Well lucky us... we had a whole caravan park of people celebrating end of ramadan... noise at night was loud and went past 10pm... car horns blaring, no security at the park monitoring noise... paying over $100 a night ... what a joke ... called office emergency line.. went to voicemail... We went away for a quiet weekend and so disappointed...i wish we were advised at time of booking and yes we only booked this week so the park would have known.
A great park close to facilities and well maintained great location
I only visited for the day to see a friend but from what I seen it was a good little park and family friendly.
Loved the service of the Marriage saver Parker  the park is beautiful. Best I've stayed at. Loved the pool and the piped in music whilst enjoying my shower and sit.
Renae and the team were fantastic. I would highly recommend this place. They saved our holiday after the car broke down and couldn't do more to accommodate us. Beautiful park and great location.
Great place to relax and do nothing,
Very friendly and helpful staff here. Great facilities for families. Plenty going on and good for fishing.

Fantastic caravan park, big sites, dog friendly and Great Lake for Kayaking, fishing, paddle boarding or just a cruise in the boat, there is a playground and swimming pool with slide for the young at heart.
Highly recommended
Great amenities, very clean sites. Staff wonderful.
Wonderful location

Beautiful caravan park we booked a premium site and were not disappointed.  Huge site with synthetic turf, spotless amenities and lovely friendly staff. Its a little way out of town, has a Cafe, lap pool, swimming pool all spotless.  We will definitely stay again.

My first time camping on astroturf it's
Flat and no bumps beautiful place.
The environment is beautiful and all the staff are very friendly. We will come again and recommend it to our friends
Loved this place, decent rooms and amazing location

Had a great time here. Very well organised activities. Have fun activities everyday. With mobile activities reminders we had fun time even when the weather wasn’t that great. Will definitely recommend to my friends .
great park

We stayed in the 3 bedroom cabin no 39 opposite the pool. 5 days before we arrived they emailed us to say that the main pool would be closed for 4 of the 6 days while we were there. Cant understand why they didnt do the maintenance during winter. Last year the price was $340 per night shoulder.  It has just gone up to $580  per night. How can people on a budget afford that? The facilities in the cabin will have to improve a lot if they are going to charge that kind of Money.

*UPDATED*

After coming here for many years, this will be our last stay. the cabin we book every year has almost doubled in price ($340 to $600 per night) and to top it all off we received an email a week out from our stay (which has been booked for a year) we were told that a large portion of the amenities will be closed!

Great location, park is very well maintained, staff are great and friendly. I couldn't fault the place, we will be back next year
We stayed on a powered site for 4 days. Fantastic location and the staff there are great 👍

A wonderful break away in this secure and fun park, that is pet friendly & with our Ensuite right next to our Van, we couldn't have wanted for anything.
Including a Cafe with delicious meals on site. Offering discount meals everyday.
Great place for the family

Probably the tidiest park we have been to, good range of cabins, sites seem very roomy though some roads seem a bit narrow but the " Marriage Saver" man is there to assist. Great Cafe, with daily specials, lap pool beside the Cafe is fantastic plus a large pool area with water park facilities. Highly recommended
Great location. Rooms need a serious refresh and bug spray

Always improving. Big sites. Clean amenities. Great for boats. Now 2023 and the park is now run by NRMA, standards of the park have dropped quite a lot, my site prices have gone up from $2165 for 6 weeks to $3385 for the same 6 weeks next year. We won't be back.
Big camping site, really good camp kitchen with plenty of furniture even 2 ovens, bathroom are clean, and the reception was really professional.

Very clean with synthetic turf on most sites, and all sites appear to be very level. Seems to be very family and estuary fisherman orientated.
If you want to enjoy a quiet stay however, this isn't the place. Lots of kids running/riding around and screaming, especially at the pool area. Many families seem to know each other, and some ignored the 10pm noise rule, partying till midnight. I felt sorry for others trying to get some sleep.
The "Marriage Saver" tractor is running throughout the day moving vans and bins around which gets annoying. They seem very over enthusiastic to use the tractor and people have to disconnect their cars in the middle of the road, then the tractor positions the van.... sometimes simply straight back onto the site. This causes a lot of congestion on the narrow roads.
There is nowhere outside or inside the park to stop when checking in. The sign says to "Press the button on the intercom and await instructions". We did that, and the boom gate opened, but nobody told us what to do. We drove in, but missed the sign that says to stop at the speed hump and check in, as we were watching the kids that were running across in front of our car. We stopped at a vacant site and checked in. The moment we drove off to our site an old man yelled at us to slow down, yet we hadn't moved even a couple of metres and didn't take off quick. Not a good start.
Due to the congestion throughout the day, some people leave their cars outside the park and walk in through the back gate.
A nice park and seems to be very popular, but not our scene.
We had a caravan spot for our RV. Great amenities, kept very clean and everyone is very friendly
The MOST expensive park.
Starting to price out of the market, needs to update security, petty theft at night, staff bedside manner needs some work.
We were a large group.  Lovely clean and well maintained holiday park.  Managers and employees were so helpful.

Cabin was nice, clean with all amenities. The location was great. Peaceful and quiet. Great access to water. Kids loved it. The pool area a real hit. The people were very friendly. The beds were far too hard for my liking, but that was biggest issue. We hope to come back again.

Not bad,lovely location,cabin was dog friendly which was nice.Cabin was clean and air-conditioned,little verandah was lovely,but would be really nice if there was a little gate to stop dog from wandering away.

Managed to convince the missus to camp for 4 nights in my tourer with a roof top tent and drop down annexe. Plenty room on the powered site( 10m x 6m from memory) all sites are astro turfed so no dead grass or dirt through you gear. Make sure you have solid pegs and good lumpy to drive them in. Staff were friendly on arrival, check in was super easy. Great pool for kids with slides and water features. Lap pool for adults. All sealed roads. Amenities were ok, but you have to remember you're in a van park with heaps of people, so don't expect the shangri la. Has a boat ramp with a long warf on ohmas bay. Great for kayaks and paddle boards. Has a cafe on site that does brekky and lunch with limited grocery supplies. They have school holiday programmes every day with street food vans and occasional live music for dinner times. Only down side is you have to drive to the beach and shops if walking isn't your thing, sorry didn't do any pictures

Stayed in a 2 bedroom lake view cabin for 3 nights. Cabin had everything we required for our stay and special mention for the great water pressure in the shower. Staff very friendly and helpful. Food trucks each night during the holidays was fantastic. Great pool and slide. Enjoyed fishing straight out from the park. Easy walk to town. Very clean and tidy park. Definitely will be returning again

tayed in a Powered van site ,parked on artificial grass so soft under the feet,level sites,super quiet at night,plenty of activities for all, ,there is bike hire,fishing on the river ,kids water park and also a swimming pool,clean amenity blocks, dump points, tractor assistance to place the van on site and close to local shops etc.For those who like an early morning cuppa or breakfast there is a Cafe on site and texts with daily specials from the Cafe,there are also meet and greet sausage sizzles,pop up pizzas etc you can attend if youde like...friendly service all round..thankyou so much enjoyed ourselves.
Location great, cabin clean with only minor issues of entry steps needing attention & very squeeky toilet door. Staff polite & helpful.
Handy location, NRMAParks great
Great park ,staff were great will stay again
Very tight
Second time at this park, they even park the van for you
One of the best van parks we have been to. Excellent facilities, spotless condition and great location
Power site for silver schoolies week fantastic, live music, entertainment, friendly helpful staff booked for next year

Previously under banner of Big4 Parks and now an NRMA Park. Can observe changes in ownership and not for the better. Out of order toilets, dryers. No daily rubbish collection from vans. Lack of communication around daily activities for park patron and cafe appears to have changed in its facilities.
Also be aware when booking sites …. Booked 12 months ahead July school holidays and was charged $85 a night. Upon talking with other campers they had only partially d $40 a night with offers to extend their stay at only $20 a night. When management questioned about discrepancies in site costs their answers was they have a dynamic cost scale. Have I re-booked for next year- not at $85 a night! Will look closer to date and online! On the positive still have Marriage Saver tractor to put vans on site and take off site…phew!! Cleaning staff amazing. Astro turf sites very well maintained.

If you like National Park style camping or camping in a natural setting this is not the camp for you. The park has many facilities for young families is well staffed and has many services such as parking your van and rubbish collection. The park is run more like a resort which has created a well run park and plenty of employment. The park sent regular text msgs about kiosk hours, free sausage sizzles, the coffee van etc. Personally it was not our cup of tea astro turf, yapping dogs and kids everywhere, people that sit in their van for hours everyday to watch TV and no view from any direction is not our idea of fun.
Beautiful park with astro turf clean stunning waterfront and great swimming pool splash park. 👌
Best holiday we had in a long while

Great spot. There is a large reserve on the waterfront. Not sure if it belongs to the caravan park or council or whatever, but it has bbqs and tables/benches allowing the park guests to watch the sunset.
All the powered sites are 100% covered in astroturf, so you get a sort of valet experience when the staff park your van with a tractor. There is an non-heated pool with a couple of small slides. Some kids were in there today. (Late June)
As seems to be the thing in NSW/QLD there is no screening/bushes between sites so there is a little compromise to privacy.
Lovely caravan park. Turf grounds. Great pool and facilities.
Great location and plenty to do... Pool was good size and had heated section. Right on the water channel so make sure you take your fishing rod..

We had hired a cabin with two bedroom with a lake view. The Lakeview from our cabin was very beautiful.The place is very family friendly. The cars move around with most caution of kids and pets. The pool was nice and the kids enjoyed the water slide. The cabin was neat and all necessary amenities were provided. There is a Woolworths 4mins drive away and that was pretty handy. The only issue we had was the internet connectivity. But if you are up for staying with no digital interruption this should not be an issue. 😊

NRMA Forster Tuncurry Holiday Park is an excellent choice to stay at allowing you to explore all of the surroundings. This area is in the heart of a beautiful part of the mid-north coast. Beaches abound in all directions. Shopping in Stocklands Forster has got everything you could want. Waterside cafes and takeaway fresh seafood shops offer tremendous locations to dine at.

Now, this park is smaller in comparison to the many NRMA parks that I have stayed at. This includes NSW and Victoria predominantly the Victorian coast line from Mallacoota round to Portland. I love this park as it has great fishing locations at your doorstep. The staff are constantly maintaining all aspects of the park. It's a real credit to all of them. The facilities are kept immaculate. It's the occasional guests who sometimes leave a bit to be desired. For what it's worth, I would highly recommend NRMA Forster Tuncurry Holiday Park to any traveller. I stay on powered sites during my travels.
Even though I don't stay there I always enjoy a coffee at their cafe. Their staff are always kind and friendly.

We stayed here mid week for 3 nights.
Cabin is very basic but fully functional.
The cabin we stayed in was by the water. We had breakfast on our porch and also down on the waterfront.
The location is close to the town centre. Amazing cafe also just outside the park selling great coffee breakfast and takeaway or dine in foods. Great menu.
The park was very relaxing. We had a lovely holiday there.
